political.s: FEC: $871K in executive political donations; partisan lean Democratic +56 across 27 executive donors.

charity.amt: Billions pledged via CZI over lifetime; ~$50M direct corporate donation noted in 2026
charity.focus: Biomedical research, AI, education, housing affordability

dei.s: Stanford FMTI Dec2025 foundation-model transparency: 31/100 — bottom-tier; substantial opacity across data, labor, compute, and downstream-use indicators.

privacy.s: Common Sense Privacy: Warning rating across 4 product(s) (Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp (+1 more)) — does not meet minimum data-protection safeguards.

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 65:1 (median employee pay $388,200) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).
execPay.ratio: ~72:1 (other NEOs vs. median worker); CEO ratio atypical due to $1 base salary

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

health.s: Child-safety concerns flagged by 5Rights Foundation / 41-state-AG suit. 41 US state AGs sued Meta Oct 2023 alleging deliberate features that harmed teen mental health; 5Rights' Pathways report identified Instagram's algo surfacing eating-disorder content to teen accounts.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Meta React Server Components Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Meta React Server Components contains a remote code execution vulnerability that could allow unauthenticated remote code execution by exploiting a flaw in how React decodes payloads sent to React Server Function endpoints. Please note CVE-2025-66478 has been rejected, but it is associated with CVE-2025- 55182.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

highestSeverity.vulnerabilityName: Meta React Server Components Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
